changes.exact_quote: If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay an exit charge equal to the full unpaid balance for that Order Form.
changes.proposed_redline: If Customer ends an Order Form without Vendor default, Customer will pay only accrued, undisputed fees for services performed before the effective date plus undisputed reimbursable expenses approved in writing.

changes.exact_quote: For purposes of this clause, the following additional term applies: Vendor will defend Customer against third-party claims only to the extent finally determined by a court to arise from Vendor's willful misconduct.
changes.proposed_redline: Each party will defend and indemnify the other from third-party claims arising from the indemnifying party's breach, unlawful conduct, or materials it provides. Vendor will also defend and indemnify Customer against claims alleging that the services or Deliverables infringe intellectual property rights.

changes.exact_quote: Liability is capped at three months of fees, including confidentiality, data-protection, indemnity, and infringement claims; claim notice is 30 days and invoices remain net 45.
changes.proposed_redline: Except for breaches of confidentiality, data protection obligations, indemnification obligations, intellectual property infringement, fraud, willful misconduct, and gross negligence, each party's aggregate liability is capped at the fees paid or payable under the applicable Order Form during the twelve months before the event giving rise to the claim.

changes.exact_quote: Vendor may amend any legal or commercial term by posting revised terms on its website, effective immediately without Customer approval.
changes.proposed_redline: No amendment, modification, or waiver is effective unless in a writing signed by authorized representatives of both parties.

summary: All eight T2-N response children reviewed against the v4 answer key.
